non_blocking_issues.description: JiraIngestionRequest in src/api/ingestion.py declares base_url, email, token, and project_key as required (Field(...)). The api-contracts.json JiraIngestionRequest schema deliberately defines NO required fields ('none of the credential fields are mandatory at the schema level') and documents that each defaults to its JIRA_* env var and that missing/invalid credentials must return HTTP 200 with a degraded IngestionSummary.errors. With the current model, a request that omits any credential (or sends null) is rejected by FastAPI with HTTP 422 instead of the contract/AC20-specified HTTP 200 + errors list. The hard safety guarantee (never HTTP 5xx) still holds, and the canonical contract example case (token="" empty string, other fields present) is handled correctly by JiraConnector._validate_credentials -> HTTP 200 + errors, which is why this is non-blocking; but absent/null fields diverge from the documented degradation behavior.
non_blocking_issues.suggestion: Make base_url/email/token/project_key Optional[str] with default None, fall back to settings.jira_base_url/jira_email/jira_token when omitted, and let JiraConnector._validate_credentials() surface missing fields as IngestionSummary.errors (HTTP 200). This also wires up the JIRA_* settings that are currently defined but unused (AC28 env-fallback intent).

non_blocking_issues.description: All ingestion test suites referenced by T-10/T-11/T-12 (tests/test_connectors.py, tests/test_ingestion_api.py, tests/test_ingestion_auth.py, tests/integration/test_ingestion_pipeline.py) are declared 'to be created by T-14-QA-INGESTION' and do not yet exist. The critical degradation paths (GitLab 401/429, Jira missing credentials, CV missing PDF backend, HR/Slack malformed rows), the auth boundaries (403 developer / 401 unauthenticated), the HTTP 413 guard, and the provenance.llm+heuristic==enriched invariant are therefore currently unverified by automated tests. Per the review rubric an untested edge case is a non-blocking issue; T-14 is the dedicated QA task that must close this before the deployment gate (test_plan.json summary.failed == 0).
non_blocking_issues.suggestion: Ensure T-14-QA-INGESTION delivers the fully-mocked, offline tests for every documented degradation and auth path and confirm summary.failed == 0 before the deployment gate.

non_blocking_issues.description: In src/connectors/jira_connector.py _fetch_all_assignees() computes a real 'email' from assignee.get('emailAddress') (or a fallback) but then discards it: the yielded SourceDocument hardcodes email=f"{uid.lower()}@jira.example" instead of using the resolved 'email' variable. The result is an unused local variable and the loss of the genuine assignee email address that was available from the Jira payload.
non_blocking_issues.suggestion: Use the already-computed 'email' value in the yielded SourceDocument (email=email) so real assignee emails are preserved and the unused variable is eliminated.

non_blocking_issues.description: AC22 is satisfied at the application-code level: src/api/ingestion.py reads the upload into memory, enforces settings.max_upload_bytes BEFORE any connector parsing (HTTP 413 on exceed), and neither the endpoint nor any connector writes to disk. Note for completeness: Starlette's UploadFile backs the multipart body with a SpooledTemporaryFile that rolls over to a temp file on disk once it exceeds its in-memory threshold, so for bodies between that threshold and MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES the framework itself may spool to disk before the size check runs. This is framework behavior outside the reviewed code and is already acknowledged in the T-11 known_limitations.
non_blocking_issues.suggestion: Optional hardening: short-circuit oversized uploads using the Content-Length header before reading the body, and/or stream-read with an incremental byte cap, to avoid any framework-level disk spooling for large rejected payloads.

non_blocking_issues.description: frontend/src/api/client.ts uploadFile() intentionally bypasses the shared request() helper (correct, so the browser can set the multipart/form-data boundary), but as a consequence it does not inherit the silent 401 -> token-refresh -> retry-once logic that all other client calls have. A token that expires mid-session will surface a hard ApiClientError(401) on file upload rather than transparently refreshing.
non_blocking_issues.suggestion: Optionally factor the refresh-on-401 retry into a small wrapper reused by uploadFile() (still letting the browser set the multipart boundary) for behavioral consistency with the rest of the client.

non_blocking_issues.description: The in-place refactor of POST /api/v1/developers (src/api/developers.py) to route creation through the shared create_developer_profile() helper changes which _enqueue_embeddings reference the create path schedules: it is now the src.core.helpers function invoked inside the helper module, not the name bound in src.api.developers. Any existing Phase-1 test that monkeypatched src.api.developers._enqueue_embeddings to assert the POST /developers create path enqueues embeddings would no longer observe that call (the PUT/update path still uses the developers-module name and is unaffected). This is a regression risk for the stated '120 existing tests must remain green' constraint that cannot be confirmed without executing the suite.
non_blocking_issues.suggestion: Run the full existing test suite (T-14/QA) and confirm 120 tests stay green; if any test patched src.api.developers._enqueue_embeddings for the create path, update it to patch src.core.helpers._enqueue_embeddings.

summary: Reviewed the Data Pipeline & Ingestion increment (T-10 connectors, T-11 ingestion API + ETL orchestrator + shared helper + settings, T-12 frontend) against api-contracts.json, architecture.json, and the task done-criteria. All core acceptance criteria are met: (AC19/AC20/AC30) all five connectors degrade gracefully and never raise/return HTTP 5xx — GitLab handles 401/429/network and always yields a degraded SourceDocument, Jira validates credentials before any network call and yields zero records + errors, HR/Slack/CV degrade per-record in memory; (AC21) require_manager is applied to every /api/v1/ingestion/* route (401 unauthenticated, 403 developer-role); (AC22) MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES is enforced before any parsing with HTTP 413 and no disk writes in endpoint/connector code; (AC16/AC23) src/core/helpers.create_developer_profile is the single create-plus-embed path imported by both src/api/developers.py and src/etl/orchestrator.py with no duplication; (AC23/AC24/AC25) the orchestrator wraps enrich_profile in asyncio.to_thread, counts empty-skills records in skipped and excludes them from creation, and maintains provenance.llm + provenance.heuristic == enriched; (AC15/AC16) preview mode persists nothing (created=0, db/background_tasks=None) while commit mode persists via the shared helper and enqueues embeddings via BackgroundTasks; (AC26) the manager-only Ingestion page guards on session.role==='manager', the ConnectorPicker disables credential-gated entries with a required-credentials tooltip, all declared data-testid attributes are present, and uploadFile() adds a multipart helper without altering existing client.ts functions. Scope is clean — changes stay within the files listed across the three code_specs (including the T-11-owned src/main.py router registration). No blocking issues found. Six non-blocking items are recorded: the most material is NBL-001, a JiraIngestionRequest schema deviation (required fields + unused JIRA_* env fallback) that makes absent-credential requests return HTTP 422 instead of the contract/AC20-specified HTTP 200 + degraded summary — the never-5xx guarantee and the canonical empty-string-token case still behave correctly, so work can proceed, but the request schema should be relaxed to match the contract. The remaining items cover pending T-14 test coverage, a discarded real Jira email, framework-level multipart spooling, the uploadFile 401-refresh gap, and a refactor-induced regression risk for the existing 120-test suite to verify in QA.
